title: Logic, Sets, Counting, and Decision Rules · Spiral Review
summary: Audit eligibility rules and resource allocation without double-counting people or contradicting policy. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a tested eligibility and allocation rule set after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent categories, subsets, unions, intersections, and complements from precise definitions.
Use Venn diagrams and inclusion-exclusion to count overlapping groups exactly once.
Distinguish statements, predicates, quantifiers, negation, converse, inverse, and contrapositive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper
